title: polygala vulgaris subsp. pseudoalpestris
intro: Polygala vulgaris subsp. pseudoalpestris, also known as Alpestris or Wild Balm, is a rare sub-species of the Polygala vulgaris plant, a flowering perennial native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Polygala vulgaris subsp. pseudoalpestris include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and promote wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant often take the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its health benefits.
